title: Lamb Skewers with Mediterranean Flair
description: A flavorful and colorful dish featuring tender lamb, marinated in a mix of herbs and spices, grilled to perfection on skewers.

ingredients.name: Lamb shoulder, cut into 1-inch cubes
ingredients.notes: You can use any cut of lamb, but shoulder works best for skewers

ingredients.name: Smoky olive oil
ingredients.notes: You can also use regular olive oil if you don't have smoky olive oil

ingredients.name: Intensely flavored feta cheese
ingredients.notes: Don't skip this ingredient, as it adds a lot of flavor to the lamb

ingredients.name: Fresh oregano
ingredients.notes: You can use dried oregano if you don't have fresh oregano

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Don't overdo it with the garlic, as it can overpower the dish

ingredients.name: Lemon juice
ingredients.notes: Freshly squeezed lemon juice works best

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use kosher salt or sea salt for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: Use freshly ground black pepper for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Wooden skewers
ingredients.notes: Make sure to soak the skewers in water for at least 30 minutes before grilling

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the olive oil, feta cheese, oregano, garlic, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb cubes to the marinade and mix well to coat.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
Thread the lamb cubes onto the skewers, leaving a little space between each piece.
Grill the skewers for 8-10 minutes per side, or until the lamb is cooked to your desired level of doneness.
Let the skewers rest for 5 minutes before serving.
